metadata.seed: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

patient.query: Yeah. Stu and I were talking about it before I came in, and I can't really figure out why I'm so bothered. We have such a great relationship. I mean, I feel lucky as a woman in the 21st century to have a man who cares about her and takes her out and makes her feel appreciative. I've never had that before. And I go home happy. I go home excited to see him. I can't name a single friend or even my sisters who have that. And still all I hear is there are criticisms, there are words, how weird it is, and why would a 58 year old man want to be with you? You're not even 30 yet, you know? And I just don't see that. I don't see age being an issue. But again, like, I'm just, I'm waking up with those thoughts.

automatic_thoughts: I can't figure out why I'm so bothered
there are criticisms about my relationship
it's weird for a 58 year old man to be with me
age is an issue for others but not for me

neutral: Fear of reaction regarding family planning choices (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel so happy and fulfilled in your relationship, and then be pulled down by these critical thoughts. Can you tell me more about where those "criticisms" and "weird" thoughts are coming from?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a real conflict – feeling incredibly fortunate and happy in your relationship with Stu, and then being pulled down by these critical thoughts about the age difference. It makes sense that you're bothered when those thoughts intrude on your positive feelings.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel so happy and fulfilled in your relationship, and then be pulled down by these critical thoughts. Can you tell me more about where those "criticisms" and "weird" thoughts are coming from?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds incredibly frustrating to feel so happy and fulfilled in your relationship with Stu, and then be weighed down by these critical thoughts about the age difference. What do you think Stu might say if he heard you describing how these thoughts are impacting you?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a real conflict between how you feel about Stu and the criticisms you're hearing – that must be really unsettling. When you said you're waking up with those thoughts, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: Yeah, it is really frustrating. I’m not sure where they come from exactly. Sometimes it feels like they just pop up out of nowhere, like a shadow creeping in when everything seems fine. It’s like I have this voice in my head that tells me I’m not good enough or that something bad is going to happen, even when I’m really happy.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not good enough
something bad is going to happen

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like these thoughts are really disruptive, especially when you're feeling happy. Can you tell me a little more about what happens right before you notice these thoughts popping up?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like these thoughts are really unsettling, especially when you're feeling happy. It takes courage to describe something like that, and I appreciate you sharing it with me.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like these thoughts are really disruptive, especially when you're feeling happy. Can you tell me a little more about what happens right before you notice these thoughts popping up?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds incredibly unsettling to experience those thoughts, especially when you're feeling happy. Can you imagine what a friend might say about those thoughts, or how they might view the situation?

safety_check.response: It sounds really frustrating to have these thoughts intrude, especially when you're feeling happy. When you say you have these thoughts, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: It’s usually when I'm with someone—like a friend or my partner. We'll be having a good time, and then suddenly, I get this nagging feeling that something's off. Like, maybe I’m not as interesting as they think, or they’ll get tired of me. It sort of ruins the moment, you know? I just wish I could enjoy things without that looming worry.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not as interesting as they think
they'll get tired of me

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds really frustrating to have that worry pop up and disrupt enjoyable moments. Can you tell me more about what makes you think you might not be as interesting as the other person thinks?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a real struggle to enjoy time with people you care about, and that worry can be really disruptive. It takes courage to share that, and it makes sense that you'd want to find a way to feel more at ease.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds really frustrating to have that worry pop up and disrupt enjoyable moments. Can you tell me more about what makes you think you might not be as interesting as the other person thinks?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds really frustrating to have that worry pop up and disrupt what should be enjoyable moments. Sometimes, when we're in the middle of a good time, it can be helpful to step back and consider what a friend might think about how you're interpreting the situation – do you think they might see things differently?

safety_check.response: That sounds really frustrating, and it makes sense you'd want to enjoy those moments without that worry. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I guess it’s just this feeling that I don't have much to offer. Like, if I’m not active or funny all the time, then maybe they’ll lose interest. I can’t help but compare myself to others; it’s like I’m constantly measuring my worth against what I think they expect. It just leaves me feeling inadequate, like I'm just pretending to be someone I’m not.

automatic_thoughts: I don't have much to offer
If I'm not active or funny, they'll lose interest
I'm constantly measuring my worth against others
I'm just pretending to be someone I'm not

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're putting a lot of pressure on yourself to be a certain way in order to be accepted – what makes you think they expect that of you?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of pressure to be a certain way, and that's creating this feeling of inadequacy and like you're not being genuine. That must be really tough to carry around.

safety_check.response: That sounds incredibly painful, feeling like you're constantly needing to prove your worth and like you're not being genuine. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: Yeah, it feels like that sometimes. I think it’s just this voice in my head, maybe from past experiences? I’ve had friends or even family who seemed to only be around when I was fun or cheerful. When I wasn’t, it felt like they drifted away. So, I guess I just worry that if I show any weakness or flaws, they'll leave too.

automatic_thoughts: if I show weakness, they'll leave

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like that experience has really shaped how you see your relationships – can you tell me more about what that voice in your head says when you feel like showing weakness?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of worry about your relationships, and it makes sense that past experiences would shape how you see things now. It takes courage to acknowledge that fear of vulnerability.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds like that experience has really shaped how you see your relationships – can you tell me more about what that voice in your head says when you feel like showing weakness?

safety_check.response: That sounds really painful, and it makes sense you'd feel worried given those experiences. When you say you've had those thoughts, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: It’s like a wave of panic washes over me. I start thinking things like, “What if they see this side of me and decide I’m not worth their time?” I can almost feel the room getting colder, like there's a distance growing. It makes me want to put on a mask—smile, joke around—just to keep that fear at bay. But then I feel so exhausted trying to keep it up.

automatic_thoughts: What if they see this side of me and decide I’m not worth their time?

neutral: Fear of speaking to people in authority (finding)
Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly draining to constantly feel that wave of panic and the need to put on a mask to manage it. It takes a lot of energy to keep that up, and it's understandable that you'd feel exhausted.

safety_check.response: That sounds incredibly draining, and it takes a lot of effort to manage those feelings. When you said you were having those thoughts, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. Sometimes, I just want to take off the mask and be myself, but the thought of what might happen if I do is terrifying. I just don't want to risk losing anyone, even if it means pretending. It feels safer that way, but it’s like I’m trapped in my own mind.

triggers: wanting to take off the mask
automatic_thoughts: I might lose someone if I show my true self
pretending feels safer
I'm trapped in my own mind

llm_response.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel trapped like that, wanting to be yourself but fearing the consequences. What's the most frightening thing that you imagine might happen if you were to take off that mask?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel like you're constantly putting on a mask, and that the fear of losing someone is holding you back from being your true self. That feeling of being trapped must be really unsettling.

socratic_questioning.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel trapped like that, wanting to be yourself but fearing the consequences. What's the most frightening thing that you imagine might happen if you were to take off that mask?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds incredibly difficult to feel so trapped and to weigh the risk of being yourself against the fear of losing someone. What do you imagine a close friend might say about you trying to be more authentic, even with the potential for discomfort?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of fear and anxiety around showing your true self, and that's completely understandable. When you said you were trapped in your own mind, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: I think the scariest part is the fear of rejection—like if they see the real me, they might not like what they find. I’ve been hurt before when I opened up, and it left this mark on me. I worry that if I show my true self, they’ll think I’m not worth sticking around for, and that thought just... it really weighs heavy on me.

automatic_thoughts: they might not like what they find
if I show my true self, they’ll think I’m not worth sticking around for

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of worry and sadness about being truly seen by someone, especially given past experiences where opening up led to hurt. That kind of fear can be really heavy to carry around.

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pain and worry about being rejected, and it's understandable that it feels so heavy. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?
